PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Meeghan Buckley for and with Norman Nickens.

The Court stated that the Probate Examiner took a look at the will and it appears to be an original, and that it will proceed on probate of an original will. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for January 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Cecilia P. Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Continued To Jan-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Continued To Dec-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Norman Nickens, Petitioner, in propria persona.

Mr. Nickens stated his father (decedent) was 98 years old and blind when he died, that his father was being cared for by his mother, his brother and himself, and when his mother died in May 2014 his father moved to San Francisco. Mr. Nickens further stated the only beneficiaries are his brother and himself and that the only asset is the checking account. The Court stated this sounds like a relatively straightforward estate, that all probate matters have a lot of requirements, encourages Mr. Nickens to obtain counsel, that having counsel will protect him with fiduciary duties, and asked if Mr. Nickens would like to amend his petition with IAEA authority, explaining that without IAEA authority we will not be able to marshal the bank account. Mr. Nickens stated that there are no bills, no creditors, that the income was from VA, that zero has come out of the account the past 4-5 years, that he does not need to amend the petition to include IAEA, that he has paid for fees out of his pocket, that there is no need to access the account, that he understands he won't be able to marshal the account until close of estate and will file an amended duties and liabilities, and provide correct notice per Probate Examiner's notes. The Court further stated that there will be additional filings after this, such as inventory and appraisal and close of estate where an attorney specializing in probate will be beneficial. Mr. Nickens stated he will discuss this with his brother. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to December 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
